title: clerodendrum tomentosum var. typicum
intro: Clerodendrum tomentosum var. typicum is a variety of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the 'Kadupul flower' or 'Queen of the night', which blooms only at night and wilts before dawn. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Clerodendrum tomentosum var. typicum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an antioxidant, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceuticals, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
